message:
perf(time/wheel): cache max duration values as u64 nanoseconds to avoid repeated u128-to-u64 conversions

Add max_wheel_duration_ns and max_timer_duration_ns as u64 fields on
the TimerWheel struct, computed once at construction time via a new
duration_to_u64_nanos() helper function. The helper calls
Duration::as_nanos() which returns u128, and clamps the result to
u64::MAX before casting.

Previously, every insert() call converted max_timer_duration to
nanoseconds for the duration bounds check, and every max_range_ns()
call converted max_wheel_duration. Both of these are hot paths:
insert() is called for every timer registration, and max_range_ns()
is called during wheel advancement. The u128 intermediate from
Duration::as_nanos() requires wider arithmetic and an extra comparison
on 64-bit platforms, which is wasteful when the values are constant
after construction.

New test:
- wheel_max_range_ns_tracks_configured_wheel_duration: verifies that
  max_range_ns() returns the correct cached nanosecond value matching
  the configured wheel duration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
